action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 23 spent ~105 minutes (likely an unintended long run) training a weak LightGBM that only reached local IC 0.015, below our single-model PyTorch baseline of 0.02045. We have only ~35 minutes and 7 iterations left. Analysis: Past LGB failures were due to severe underfitting (30 trees, default leaves). A properly tuned LGB (many leaves, lower learning rate, thousands of rounds) is the best remaining shot to beat the baseline, because tree ensembles can capture non-linear feature interactions that MLPs miss. If this fails, we still retain the 0.02045 baseline. Decision: Write a clean, high-capacity LGB training script (train_lgb_v2.py) with strong hyperparameters, save to a new directory (checkpoints/lgb_strong), and immediately run local predict.py to measure IC. Chain both commands in one iteration to save time. Only submit official eval in the next iteration if local IC exceeds 0.02045.
action.command: python train_lgb_v2.py && python predict.py --data_path local_test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/lgb_strong --output local_test_eval/lgb_strong.json
